gap_summaries.title: burn_in_check parse_test_names regex only matches pytest-style output
gap_summaries.description: The regex r'^(\S+::\S+)\s+(PASSED|FAILED|ERROR)' only matches pytest verbose output format. When users configure a non-pytest burn_in_cmd (e.g. vitest, jest), the parser captures no test names, detect_flaky returns empty, and the check silently passes regardless of flakiness. Silent false-negative violates fail-closed principle for non-pytest test runners.
gap_summaries.rationale: Test quality checks document pytest as the primary supported runner. Users of other runners can customize burn_in_cmd to produce pytest-compatible output, or the category can be set to N/A. Future milestone can add multi-runner parser support.

gap_summaries.title: _evals_cmd does not validate custom eval_cmd list elements
gap_summaries.description: When profile.rules.agentic.eval_cmd is a list, elements are passed through without validating they are non-empty strings. Non-string elements would cause subprocess failures at runtime rather than at command-build time.
gap_summaries.rationale: Single-operator threat model — profile is authored by the trusted operator. The collector runner's fail-closed error handling (status='error' evidence) catches any subprocess failures. Validation at build_cmd time would be defense-in-depth but is not required for v1.

gap_summaries.title: Inner subprocess timeouts in check scripts do not honor profile timeouts
gap_summaries.description: burn_in_check.py has timeout=600 and mutation_check.py has timeout=1800 hardcoded inside the check scripts. These do not honor profile.timeouts[category]. The outer collector_runner timeout takes precedence (SIGKILL via psutil), so the inner timeouts are defense-in-depth only. No correctness impact.
gap_summaries.rationale: The outer collector runner timeout (resolved from profile) is the authoritative timeout. Inner script timeouts prevent orphan processes when the outer timeout fires. The hardcoded values are reasonable defaults for their respective operations.

gap_summaries.title: check_score has unreachable branch when called from main
gap_summaries.description: check_score's 'if not review' branch (line 31-32) is unreachable from main() because main() handles empty review at line 58-59 with a graceful pass (exit 0) before calling check_score. The function is correct as a standalone utility but the branch is dead code from the main call path.
gap_summaries.rationale: Defensive programming — the function is a reusable utility and the guard is correct for direct callers. No fix needed.

verdict: PASS — no correctness bugs found, 4 low/info production-readiness items accepted
